title: Giant Rashba Splitting and Enhanced Nonlinear Berry-Phase Responses in Sliding-Tunable vdW MXene Heterostructures
authors: Ali Sufyan, J. Andreas Larsson, Andreas Kreisel, Erik van Loon
abstract: Chalcogen-terminated van der Waals MXenes (M2CX2; M = Nb, Ta; X = S, Se) provide a robust platform for exploring strong spin-orbit coupling and proximity engineering. To probe their tunability and guide optimization of emergent properties, we systematically examine sister compounds and propose M2CS2/CrBr3 heterostructures that break time-reversal symmetry via proximity exchange coupling, enabling combined intrinsic magnetic and mechanical control.   First-principles calculations reveal Rashba splitting up to 2.53 eV A and valley-contrasting spin polarization in monolayers. These features drive strong second-order nonlinear responses, with pristine bilayer Ta2CS2 reaching a shift current of |sigma|_max approx 5 A mA/V^2 and Nb2CS2/CrBr3 attaining |D|_max approx 18.44 A. In M2CS2/CrBr3 heterostructures, the ferromagnetic substrate induces a magnetization-reversible proximity exchange field with valley-selective conduction-band renormalization (Delta_val approx 50 meV). Crucially, interfacial geometry, controlled by stacking inversion and lateral sliding, acts as a mechanical knob that continuously tunes the exchange-SOC interplay and bandgap, driving an emergent quantum anomalous Hall phase in the bilayer.
summary: The study demonstrates that sliding and stacking MXene/CrBr3 heterostructures can mechanically tune spin-orbit coupling and exchange fields. This control enables large nonlinear optical responses and the realization of a quantum anomalous Hall phase.
main_problem: Investigating the tunability of spin-orbit coupling, Rashba splitting, and nonlinear Berry-phase responses in chalcogen-terminated MXene heterostructures.
main_result: Discovery of giant Rashba splitting (up to 2.53 eV A) and strong second-order nonlinear responses, including a shift current and the emergence of a quantum anomalous Hall phase via mechanical sliding.
model_or_system: Van der Waals MXene heterostructures (M2CX2; M = Nb, Ta; X = S, Se) and M2CS2/CrBr3 heterostructures involving a ferromagnetic substrate.
key_observables: Rashba splitting, valley-contrasting spin polarization, shift current, magnetization-reversible proximity exchange field, and bandgap.
important_parameters: Rashba splitting magnitude (2.53 eV A), shift current (|sigma|_max approx 5 A mA/V^2), and valley-selective conduction-band renormalization (Delta_val approx 50 meV).
paper_structure: The paper examines sister compounds of MXenes, proposes specific heterostructures with CrBr3, and analyzes the interplay between exchange coupling and interfacial geometry (sliding/stacking) using first-principles calculations.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
